definition: Fiscal deficits simultaneously support near-term corporate earnings via the Kalecki-Levy accounting identity (~$400-600B annually flowing to aggregate profits at 6.5% deficit/GDP) and drive term premium higher via $2T+ annual Treasury issuance — a single fiscal cause producing two time-separated effects.
evidence: Kalecki-Levy accounting identity is mathematical fact
~$15-25 of ~$250-260 S&P EPS attributable to deficit (direction high-confidence, magnitude moderate)
$2T+ annual issuance pushes term premium to 80-130bp adjusted
Kalecki-Levy paradox: austerity would be equity-positive because P/E expansion dominates EPS decline at current deficit levels
open_questions: What is the precise EPS attribution — could be $5-40 range?
At what deficit/GDP level does the P/E expansion from deficit reduction dominate the EPS decline?
How does the timing arbitrage between the two effects create tradeable opportunities?
